items.title: Doctors fear new hospital design has 'increased risk of death' for women

children.content: Doctors say parts of the proposed design for Adelaide's $3.2 billion new Women's and Children's Hospital raise "significant concerns" including the location of the women's assessment service.

gallery.altText: A woman with shoulder-length blonde hair and glasses looks ahead.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with shoulder-length blonde hair and glasses looks ahead.

items.title: Adelaide motorists fined nearly $700,000 at new 40km/h school zones

children.content: The effectiveness of new speed limit signage has been questioned after the RAA revealed 1,100 people had been fined in just over one school term at two new 40km/h zones.

image.altText: Illuminated speed limit sign that says 40

items.title: SA Police search waterways for knives linked to alleged murder of Caleb Leriano

children.content: SA Police will search waterways in Adelaide's south today to find three knives they believe are connected to the alleged murder of 24-year-old Caleb Leriano.

image.altText: A young man with glasse smiles at the camera in a bright room

items.title: Premier says algal bloom 'gone' despite expansion of fishing restrictions

children.content: Fishing restrictions will be strengthened in South Australian waters because of the algal bloom's devastating impact on stocks — but the government says the bloom itself has "essentially cleared up" along most of the state's coast.

gallery.altText: Professor Mike Steer speaking to the media in the foreground with Premier Peter Malinauskas in the background
Brown foam on the shores of Glenelg.
A dead, dry fish on sand with the sea in the background

thumbnail.altText: Professor Mike Steer speaking to the media in the foreground with Premier Peter Malinauskas in the background
Brown foam on the shores of Glenelg.
A dead, dry fish on sand with the sea in the background

items.title: Greyhound Racing SA employees fired after investigation into alleged wagering activity

children.content: Greyhound Racing SA says two of its employees from the integrity team have been stood down after an investigation into alleged wagering activity.

image.altText: Eight greyhounds race down a sand track at Cannington.

items.title: Mother's impassioned plea after young daughter's cerebral palsy diagnosis

children.content: Ayla doesn't let disability slow her down, but her mother wants her to have the same opportunities as others.

image.altText: A girl in a blue and white dress, with pink glasses smiling while sitting in a park

items.title: Eyre Peninsula endures 'slow' Easter school holidays as fuel costs take a toll

children.content: Fuel costs force down tourism numbers in some typically busy South Australian towns across Easter and the school holidays.

image.altText: A man standing in front of a group of caravans.

items.title: Wheelchair user calls for 'common sense' after using accessible zone without permit

children.content: Disability advocate and wheelchair user Shane Hryhorec is calling for Adelaide Airport to use a common sense approach after being threatened with a fine for using an accessible pick-up zone without a permit.

image.altText: A man in a wheelchair with his service dog on his lap

items.title: Subdivisions taking place in Adelaide's 'visionary' Hills Face Zone

children.content: The Hills Face Zone was legislated to protect the natural character of Adelaide's landscape backdrop but, despite subdivisions not being permitted, there have been 11 over the past decade.

image.altText: Face of hillside stretching into distance with some houses among trees

items.title: Police say body found on Fleurieu Peninsula was murder victim

children.content: SA Police say initial investigations suggest a person whose body has been found in bushland south of Adelaide was murdered.

image.altText: Two police officers walk on a steeply sloped dirt road among trees

items.title: This plant is the only known wild specimen in the world, found on just 300m of roadside

children.content: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild, in crushed limestone on the side of a road. But scientists are hunting for more.

image.altText: The small fuzzy white flower of the Woods Well Spyridium

items.title: Bitumen standards lowered after supply caught in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Drivers could soon be travelling on roads built for US and European climates, as supply disruptions force governments to change the standard of bitumen used in Australia.

image.altText: Three workers in high-vis clothing fill a pothole with bitumen behind a truck.

items.title: 108yo and 103yo WWII veterans meet after more than 80 years

children.content: Two of Australia's oldest veterans served in the same place at the same time during World War II, and after the fighting, both headed home to resume their lives in SA — but the 108-year-old and the 103-year-old had never met until recently.

gallery.altText: Two elderly men seated at a table set for a lunch with a paper program

thumbnail.altText: Two elderly men seated at a table set for a lunch with a paper program

items.title: The donkey marching for the memory of all animals that serve in war

children.content: Penny the donkey, adorned in bright purple poppies, is spreading the Anzac Day legacy not just in Australia, but also in Cheshire in England.

image.altText: A grey donkey face up close in a paddock

items.title: Bupa patients lose out on choice and risk out-of-pockets under No Gap scheme, experts warn

children.content: Leaked contracts obtained by the ABC reveal Bupa's deals with private hospitals impose stifling conditions on operators that are too small to push back.

image.altText: Bupa shop front on a suburban street

items.title: Search for missing 5yo enters third day as helicopters, heat drones deployed

children.content: Helicopters, heat sensor drones and dozens of people combing through thick scrubland have been deployed in the increasingly desperate search to find missing five-year-old girl Sharon Granites.

articles.title: Criminal history of man police are searching for over missing 5yo girl revealed

children.content: A man being sought in connection with the alleged abduction of five-year-old Sharon Granites near Alice Springs has a long history of violence and prison time in the Northern Territory.

image.altText: A mugshot of an Aboriginal man, serious expression looking directly at camera, yellow / orange pigment behind brown eyes.

gallery.altText: young girl in blue shirt sits in chair holding arms out front

thumbnail.altText: young girl in blue shirt sits in chair holding arms out front

items.title: AI architects become their own victims

children.content: Tim Cook helped usher in a communications revolution and his departure from Apple marks a turning point for the company. It also serves as a reminder of how AI is changing the tech landscape.

image.altText: Tim Cook stands in front of a screen with an Apple logo.

items.title: At least four killed and dozens injured in train collision near Jakarta

children.content: The train operator says the crash, on the outskirts of Jakarta, involved a commuter train and a long-distance train.

image.altText: Dozens of emergency service workers on a train platform.

items.title: Taylor's $800m pitch to double fuel reserves to come at a cost to drives

children.content: Australia would have 60 days' worth of fuel supplies in reserve under an $800 million Coalition proposal that would cost drivers one cent per litre.

image.altText: Angus Taylor speaking to media standing outside a building.

items.title: Community divided over $1.5bn waste-to-energy incinerator proposal

children.content: A proposed waste-to-energy incinerator near Beaudesert has divided the Scenic Rim community. Supporters say it could ease Queensland's landfill crisis, while opponents fear health and environmental risks.

image.altText: An artist impression of an incinerator

items.title: They were the first victims of the Port Arthur massacre. Their family has never forgotten

children.content: Seascape guesthouse owners Sally and David Martin were the first people murdered at Port Arthur in 1996. On the 30th anniversary of the tragedy, their granddaughter tells of how the family has refused to be defined by it.

gallery.altText: A man and woman pose for a photo in front of a waterfall.

thumbnail.altText: A man and woman pose for a photo in front of a waterfall.

items.title: Aussie rugby may benefit from copying NZ's lateral thinking approach

children.content: Thinking outside the square has previously served Australian rugby well, which is why there are lessons it can take away from at least one of New Zealand's teams in Super Rugby Pacific.

image.altText: Leicester Fainga'anuku is tackled during the round 11 Super Rugby match between Crusaders and NSW Waratahs.

items.title: This basket isn't real, but it might affect your mortgage repayments

children.content: There are some key moments coming up for the Australian economy and they could make a big difference to your bank account. Emily Stewart — aka Sensible Emily — is here to help you understand what to look out for.

image.altText: GRAPHIC Basket of goods on green

items.title: The best ways to prevent common credit and debit card fraud

children.content: Experts reveal the most common ways fraudsters steal card details and how to keep your accounts safer.

gallery.altText: A woman fans out three credit cards.

thumbnail.altText: A woman fans out three credit cards.

items.title: The moment Andy Lee feared exploits with Hamish Blake had gone too far

children.content: Andy Lee opens up on his 23-year working relationship with his "fast and loose" comedy partner Hamish Blake, and the stunt that left him fearing for his mate's life.

image.altText: Hamish Blake and Andy Lee are doubled over laughing as they wear the same shirt

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

image.altText: Four men in seagull masks screech on stage with posters in the background. One has a black, yellow and red flag

items.title: Two Marios behind a Melbourne institution celebrate its 40th birthday

children.content: It may have once denied Jerry Seinfield a booking, but this Fitzroy cafe has won no shortage of fans during its four decades.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of two Marios under an umbrella in their early days

items.title: Court battle between Musk and Altman to reveal power struggle at Open AI

children.content: The world's richest man is accusing the company and its chief executive Sam Altman, and Microsoft of betraying the AI start up's original mission.

items.title: Lebanon and Hezbollah at odds over peace talks with Israel as strikes continue

children.content: Lebanon's president has accused Hezbollah of hypocrisy, as the Iranian-backed militant group demanded the government walk away from peace talks with Israel.

image.altText: Joseph Aoun stands wearing a suit and speaking at a podium.

items.title: Farmer rescues kangaroo from muddy dam amid 'perilous' drought conditions

children.content: Photos show a kangaroo moments before being rescued from a bone-dry dam in Central West NSW, after it became bogged while searching for water in an increasingly barren landscape.

gallery.altText: A close up of a kangaroo trapped in a dry dram

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a kangaroo trapped in a dry dram

items.title: Josh Hazlewood crushes Capitals in IPL bowling clinic

children.content: Australian pace maestro Josh Hazlewood delivers his best performance of the IPL season, taking four wickets to spearhead another RCB win.

image.altText: Josh Hazlewood holds up his hand

items.title: CFMEU administrator Mark Irving KC resigns

children.content: Mark Irving KC says the next "rebuild phase" of the union requires someone with "a different set of skills".

image.altText: Man with suit and black glasses walks out of court

items.title: Citizen's arrest halts alleged tiny home thief in Queensland

children.content: A chance sighting of a social media post detailing the theft of two tiny homes leads to two friends making a dramatic citizen's arrest on the Sunshine Coast.
